//: MiOS Oh-My-Posh theme.
DETERMINISTIC TWO-LINE LAYOUT (2026-05-10 redesign): the prior
left+right alignment scheme overflowed to a 3rd line on 80x20
terminals because oh-my-posh's right-block alignment math saw
the trailing 6-NBSP buffer + right_margin as part of the right
block's render width and triggered an early wrap. Operator-
reported as 'powerlines wrap incorrectly' with screenshot.
New layout:
Line 1 (block 1): leader cap + shell + path + git + exectime
+ WSL/OS indicator + HH:MM time, all packed
LEFT-aligned -- no right alignment, no
cross-block math, no wrap risk.
Line 2 (block 2): closer cap + chevron prompt arrow.
All segments still pull glyphs from mios.toml [theme.prompt]
via the post-install template substitution in
Install-MiOSOhMyPoshTheme. Palette: mios.toml [colors] (Hokusai +
operator neutrals). Language segments (node/python/go/rust/dotnet)
and conditional infrastructure segments (kubectl/aws/battery)
removed -- they pollute the line and rarely render meaningfully
on the dev VM. Operators who need them can add via mios.html.
Powerline cap glyph: U+E0B4 (rounded right half-circle). Geist
Mono Nerd Font has full Powerline coverage; the previous design
used "" (empty) which produced abutting square color blocks --
operator-flagged 2026-05-10 'pipelin isn't rounded anymore'.

segments.//: Leading prompt cap. Box-drawing chars (U+256D / U+2500) are guaranteed in any monospace font; the U+E0B6 rounded-cap PUA approach we tried in v1 was unreliable on operator's GeistMono Nerd Font Mono build (cap glyph never surfaced, leaving cols 0-2 blank).

segments.template: {{ .UpstreamIcon }}{{ .HEAD }}{{ if .BranchStatus }} {{ .BranchStatus }}{{ end }}{{ if .Working.Changed }} ✎{{ .Working.String }}{{ end }}{{ if and (.Working.Changed) (.Staging.Changed) }} |{{ end }}{{ if .Staging.Changed }}<#DC271B> +{{ .Staging.String }}</>{{ end }}

segments.//: Compact OS indicator -- just the platform glyph, no 'WSL at' prose. Saves cells; the framed dashboard already names the host kind explicitly.
Closer cap + prompt arrow. Status-tinted: tints the whole `╰─ ❯` red on non-zero exit code (.Code > 0), pale-blue otherwise (matches the leading `╭─` cap for visual symmetry).
